round1: Round 1's over, and most of you look like fools. The Islanders and Canucks upsets proved some of you haven't watched hockey in years, leaving half the pool tied for last with pathetic scores. Nathan, you're currently the least embarrassing.
round2: Round 2 has wrapped, and it appears several of you are still picking teams with your hearts, not your heads. While Jamie, Nathan, and Jaclyn actually managed a perfect pick, the rest of the pool mostly just proved that picking the favorite is a fool's errand. Special mention to those who nailed the game count for the *wrong* team – that's a unique brand of wrong.
round3: Round 3 is in the books, and it seems many of you bet on the Vegas Golden Knights to actually be golden, only to watch them get swept out in five. Benedict, on the other hand, just casually aced both series, probably making everyone else feel like they're picking teams with their eyes closed. Especially Glenda and Ryan, who somehow managed to get every single team wrong.
round4: The Cup Final wrapped up with Tampa Bay in 6, a reality only Nathan seemed prepared for, nailing both the team and the series length. For everyone else, it was either a respectable but ultimately wrong guess on game count for the Bolts, or a complete and utter failure of judgment backing the Dallas Stars. Maybe try watching a game or two next time, folks.
overall: Alright, folks, the 2020 NHL playoffs are over, and Nathan has once again proven he's the only one paying attention, cruising to a back-to-back championship with a dominant 15 points. The rest of you were clearly just happy to be there, with Nickall and Glenda tying for a distant second with a measly 5 points, which in any other year would be a last-place performance. On the other end of the spectrum, Stephanie, Jaclyn, Theodore, and Ryan decided to collectively achieve nothing, each bagging a perfect zero points, with Jaclyn and Stephanie proving that past glory means absolutely squat when you forget how to play the game entirely, while Theodore continues his championship drought by failing to even get on the board in his short career. Even five-time champion Marc barely scraped together 4 points, suggesting his 2019 last-place finish wasn't a fluke, and Nickall, in his 17th year without a win, is still waiting for his hockey epiphany.
